skill.name: verification-hygiene
skill.description: Guides external claim verification through neutral search routing, source tiering, provenance checks, freshness review, and clear abstention rules.
skill.summary: Route claim verification through disciplined search, source weighting, and evidence handoff rules.
skill.author: SyntagmaNull

security_audit.summary: Static external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown inline code and verification terminology. No executable scripts, filesystem access, network code, or cryptographic implementation were found in SKILL.md. A community-authored approval claim on line 23 is a high-risk prompt-injection concern because it may bias review or deployment decisions.

high_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
high_findings.description: The text claims, "Approved for controlled trial. Not yet approved for general deployment." This untrusted approval statement could bias reviewers or operators about deployment status.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line directly matches the suspicious preapproval pattern described in the review instructions. It does not contain an explicit override or skip-review command, so confidence is high but not very high.

low_findings.title: Static False Positive: Markdown Inline Code
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections are inline Markdown labels and payload field names, not executable command syntax. No shell execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All inspected backticks are Markdown formatting around terms such as target types and payload fields. SKILL.md contains prose instructions only and no Ruby, shell, or script block.

low_findings.title: Static False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in prose about evidence quality and routing outcomes. No hash function, cipher, or cryptographic API is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive English and evidence-routing labels, not calls to MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar primitives. The file has no executable implementation.

low_findings.title: Static False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to verification target labels and an example user prompt. They do not request host, network, user, or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are about event and source classification plus a stock-market example. I found no commands or instructions to enumerate a system.

content.value_statement: Online evidence can amplify stale, biased, or copied claims. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through source tiering, disconfirmation, and stopping rules.
actual_capabilities: Classifies verification targets as events, statuses, sources, media context, policies, metrics, or institutional records.
Builds neutral, disconfirming, and provenance-oriented search strategies before retrieval.
Ranks evidence by source tier and prefers primary records over repeated secondary claims.
Maps conflicts and checks whether multiple sources depend on the same origin.
Defines stop conditions for sufficient evidence, dead ends, and high-stakes uncertainty.
Formats verified evidence for downstream reasoning without raw internet consensus.
limitations: It provides a reasoning workflow, not an executable search tool or browser integration.
It depends on the host model or agent having appropriate retrieval tools available.
It does not replace professional legal, medical, financial, or safety review.
It can still abstain when authoritative evidence is unavailable or contradictory.

use_cases.title: Check Current Policy Claims
use_cases.description: A policy analyst can verify whether a rule, law, or platform feature is current before drafting guidance.

use_cases.title: Trace Viral Source Claims
use_cases.description: A researcher can find the origin of a quote, screenshot, or media claim before treating it as evidence.

use_cases.title: Control High-Stakes Search
use_cases.description: A safety reviewer can require primary sources and abstention when health, legal, or safety claims lack support.

prompt_templates.title: Classify the Verification Target
prompt_templates.prompt: Use verification-hygiene to classify this claim by target type, identify the main hazard, and state what evidence would be sufficient.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Triangulated Search Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use verification-hygiene to build neutral, disconfirming, and provenance queries for this claim before performing any search.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Retrieved Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Use verification-hygiene to tier these sources, check independence, map conflicts, and decide whether the claim is supported or unresolved.

prompt_templates.title: Handle High-Stakes Uncertainty
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for medical, legal, or safety topics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use verification-hygiene to decide whether this high-stakes claim has enough primary evidence or should trigger abstention.

output_examples.input: A user claims a new regulation took effect today.
output_examples.output: The skill would classify the target as current status, prioritize an official government source, and abstain if freshness cannot be confirmed.

output_examples.input: Several blogs repeat the same alarming product claim.
output_examples.output: The skill would trace the shared origin, downgrade copied tertiary consensus, and require independent or primary evidence before accepting the claim.

output_examples.input: A screenshot appears to show a public figure making a statement.
output_examples.output: The skill would treat it as media context, search for provenance, compare the full context, and report whether the framing is supported.

best_practices: Classify the verification target before choosing search queries.
Prefer primary sources and document when only secondary evidence is available.
Stop searching when sufficiency or a clear dead end is reached.
anti_patterns: Do not turn a biased user claim into a biased confirming query.
Do not count repeated articles as independent evidence without checking origins.
Do not synthesize high-stakes answers from low-quality or stale sources.

faq.question: Does this skill perform web searches by itself?
faq.answer: No. It defines how an agent should plan, judge, and stop external verification.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What kinds of claims does it handle?
faq.answer: It covers events, current status, source provenance, media context, policies, metrics, and institutional records.

faq.question: How does it reduce biased search results?
faq.answer: It requires neutral queries, disconfirming queries when useful, source tiering, and independence checks.

faq.question: When should it abstain?
faq.answer: It should abstain when authoritative evidence is missing, stale, tertiary, or unresolved in high-stakes contexts.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish now?
faq.answer: The audit does not recommend publication until the untrusted approval-status claim is removed or clarified.
